ORDER GRANTING APPLICATION OF PLAINTIFF JOHN HANCOCK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY (USA) FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IN SUPPORT OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR THE ISSUANCE OF A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - DUE TO THE ABSENCE OF ANY FILINGS SINCE MAY 14, THE TENTATIVE RULING FROM MAY 14 IS REINSTATED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. MR. PIPKINS IS BARRED FROM TRANSFERRING ANY OF THE STRUCTURED SETTLEMENT PAYMENTS RELATED TO ANNUITY CONTRACTS ISSUED BY JOHN HANCOCK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, INCLUDING ANNUITY CONTRACT 3221324, TO THIRD PARTIES UNTIL THE JUDGMENT IN THE FEDERAL CASE TITLED JOHN HANCOCK LIFE INSURANCE CO. V. DONALD C. PIPKINS, ACTION NO. 3-11-CV-04992-CRB, IS SATISFIED. FUTURE STRUCTURED SETTLEMENT PAYMENTS RELATED TO ANNUITY CONTRACTS ISSUED BY JOHN HANCOCK TO WHICH MR. PIPKINS IS ENTITLED MAY BE APPLIED TO THE JUDGMENT UNTIL IT IS SATISFIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-11-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Aug-15-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-20-2013 Continued To Aug-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Michelle Meyers, Esq. for Plaintiff; appearance by Donald Pipkins, Defendant. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to AUG-20-2013 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Mtn For Court Order Requiring John Hancock To Release Accelerated Annuity
Off Calendar May-09-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Donald C. Pipkins, defendant and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Michelle Meyers, Esq. of Burnham Brown, Tel. 510.835.6805, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: Court ordered parties to meet and confer. The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court modfied its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DONALD PIPKINS' Motion For Court Order Requiring John Hancock To Release Accelerated Annuity is denied without prejudice. Defendant fails to cite any legal basis for his motion and the relief sought is contrary to Cal. Ins. Code 10134 et seq. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion For Order Requiring John Hancock To Release Accelerated Annuity And Copies Of Award Letter To Donald Pipkins
Off Calendar Aug-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS M. DOWNEY OF BURNHAM & BROWN, TEL. NO. (510) 835-6716 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
DONALD PIPKINS & LORNA PIPKINS, TEL. NO. 821-6339, DEFENDANTS APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DONALD PIPKINS' Motion For Order Requiring John Hancock To Release Accelerated Annuity And Copies Of Award Letter To Donald Pipkins is Denied. The motion is procedurally and substantively defective. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Money is due in the future and the court has no authority to accelerate payments. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/

Hearing On Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application For The Issuance Of A Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas M. Downey, Esq., from Burnham Brown, 510-835-6716, for plaintiff and moving party John Hancock Life Insurance Company
Donald Pipkins, defendant/respondent in Pro Per, 415-821-6339

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
Hearing On Plaintiff'S Ex Parte Application For The Issuance Of A Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction - Due to the absence of any filings since May 14, the tentative ruling from May 14 is reinstated as follows: Granted. Mr. Pipkins is barred from transferring any of the structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock Life Insurance Company, including annuity contract 3221324, to third parties until the judgment in the federal case titled John Hancock Life Insurance Co. v. Donald C. Pipkins, action no. 3-11-CV-04992-CRB, is satisfied. Future structured settlement payments related to annuity contracts issued by John Hancock to which Mr. Pipkins is entitled may be applied to the judgment until it is satisfied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
